data.title: AerCap leased, purchased and sold 146 assets in Q3
data.description:
• AerCap Holdings N.V. (NYSE:AER) has signed 66 lease agreements, including 14 widebody aircraft, 25 narrowbody aircraft, 15 engines and 12 helicopters in the third quarter of 2025.
  • The company completed 35 purchases for 17 aircraft (including 8 Airbus A320neo Family aircraft and 9 Boeing 737 MAX aircraft for AerCap's owned aircraft portfolio), 11 engines for AerCap's owned engine portfolio and 7 helicopters.
  • The company also completed 45 sale transactions for 40 aircraft (including 2 Airbus A320 Family aircraft, 10 Airbus A320neo Family aircraft, 1 Airbus A330, 5 Boeing 737NGs, 7 Boeing 777-300ERs and 5 Embraer E190/E195s from AerCap's owned aircraft portfolio and 10 aircraft from AerCap's managed aircraft portfolio), 4 engines (including 1 engine from AerCap's owned engine portfolio and 3 engines from AerCap's managed engine portfolio) and 1 helicopter.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GameSquare begins $5M stock buyback program with initial repurchase
data.description:
• GameSquare Holdings (NASDAQ:GAME) repurchased 833,124 shares for $599,148.
  • The average repurchase price was ~$0.7192 per share.
  • $4.4M remains under the company's $5M buyback authorization.
  • Repurchases are funded through GameSquare’s treasury strategy.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Bruker secures $10M in federally funded NMR orders from NYSBC, Delaware, and Northwestern
Former Federal Reserve official believes the Fed should not be slashing rates
data.description:
In an opinion piece published on Friday, former New York Fed President Bill Dudley said he was not sure that the Federal Reserve continuing their easing cycle would be a good idea.

Dudley in the Bloomberg opinion column, noted that the arguments for slashing interest rates come under three buckets:

  1. The first one being risk management. Dudley said that Fed Chair Jerome Powell has said that the upside risk to inflation does not outweigh the downside risk to the labor market. However, Dudley said he is not convinced by this logic. He believes the inflation is the greater risk. "The Fed has exceeded its 2% inflation target for more than 4.5 years and is missing that target by a greater margin than its employment objective," Dudley added. He also believes while the pass-through of tariffs into prices has been muted, it is far from over, and that the monetary policy is not that restrictive. 
  2. The second is anticipation. If the Fed takes a wait-and-see approach, especially in terms of the labor market, then it might get too late. Dudley agrees that the policy should be preemptive, but only if there is enough confidence in one's outlook. "Right now, the economic outlook is highly uncertain: it’s impossible to know whether to worry more about inflation becoming entrenched and inflation expectations less well-anchored or about the labor market deteriorating substantially."
  3. The final one is estimation error. "By this logic, which Fed governor Stephen Miran has espoused, monetary policy is actually much tighter than the Fed thinks, because the neutral interest rate—the rate that neither damps nor stimulates growth—has fallen considerably," Dudley said. He questions this logic and, apart from the point on population growth, said the rest is selective at best. 

Dudley added that while the Fed has plenty of reason to worry, it does not have enough to act. He believes that the labor market is a legitimate source of concern, especially when it deteriorates beyond a certain threshold. 

He also believes that the Fed will cut the interest rates once again in the upcoming meeting. "But I doubt this is the right course or should foreshadow a long easing cycle. I’d favor a more cautious approach until the economic outlook becomes less cloudy."

data.title: Ovid Therapeutics jumps 28% on $175M private placement
data.description:
• Ovid Therapeutics (NASDAQ:OVID) entered into a securities purchase agreement for a private investment in public equity financing that is expected to result in gross proceeds of up to $175M, including initial gross proceeds of ~$81M.
  • The PIPE financing is expected to close on or about October 6, 2025.
  • The company is selling an aggregate of 57,722 shares of its Series B non-voting convertible preferred stock and warrants. The shares of Series B Preferred Stock are convertible into an aggregate of 57.72M shares of the company’s common stock.
  • Net proceeds together with the company’s existing cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, to provide financing for research and development, general corporate expenses, and working capital needs.
  • The company expects that its cash will fund its operating plan into 2028.
  • The stock price gained 26% to $2.07 per share on Friday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Massive fire at Chevron's Los Angeles-area refinery said to be contained
data.description:
Police say a massive fire that broke out at Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) El Segundo refinery near Los Angeles late Thursday has been contained.

It is not yet clear what caused the explosion at the facility, which supplies jet fuel for the Los Angeles International Airport, and supplies 20% of all motor vehicle fuels and 40% of the jet fuel consumed in southern California; the refinery is Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) second-largest in the U.S.

The fire, which required the attention of multiple fire departments, has closed several roads surrounding the refinery but flights at LAX, which is located near the site, continue to operate as normal.

The fire erupted in the refinery's Isomax 7 unit, which converts mid-distillate fuel oil into jet fuel, but it is not known how it would affect the refinery's ability to produce jet fuel and whether wider output had been affected.

Analysts say the fire is unlikely to have a large impact on the wider oil market, but California gasoline prices could rise as the state's fuel market is isolated from other U.S. refining centers in the Midwest and on the Gulf Coast.

data.title: Uranium Energy launches public offering of 15.5M common shares
Munich airport briefly halts flights after drone sightings
data.description:
Germany's Munich Airport briefly grounded 17 flights after several drone sightings overnight, impacting nearly 3,000 passengers, the latest in a string of similar incidents affecting airports in Europe.

In addition, 15 arriving flights were diverted to Stuttgart, Nuremberg, Vienna, and Frankfurt, Munich Airport said in a statement. The airport resumed flight operations early Thursday.

"When a drone is sighted, the safety of travelers is the top priority," Munich Airport noted. "It is important to emphasize that the detection and defense against drones are sovereign tasks and are the responsibility of the federal and state police."

It remains unclear how many drones were sighted, where they'd come from, and who operated them.

Similar sightings had disrupted airports in Denmark and Norway last week, just days after a cyberattack hit check-in systems at various European airports. Last month, Poland shot down Russian drones in its airspace, while three Russian fighter jets entered Estonian airspace.

data.title: Cango produces 616.6 bitcoin in September
data.description:
• Cango (NYSE:CANG) has produced 616.6 bitcoin in September comapred to 663.7 in August, bringing total number of bitcoin held to 5,810.0.
  • The deployed hashrate is 50 EH/s and average operating hashrate is 44.85 EH/s.
  • CANG +2.4% premarket to $4.67
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Huawei used Samsung, TSM and SK hynix's parts in its advanced AI chips: report
data.description:
Huawei Technologies used advanced components from Asia’s largest technology companies in at least some of its advanced Ascend AI processors, Bloomberg News reported, citing TechInsights.

TechInsights discovered gear from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM), Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) and SK hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF) in several samples of Huawei’s third-generation Ascend 910C chips, the report added.

Huawei, Samsung, SK hynix and TSM did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

In a probe, the researchers concluded that TSM manufactured the dies used in the Huawei accelerators. They also saw an older-generation type of high-bandwidth memory, called HBM2E, made by Samsung and SK Hynix. Components from the two producers were found in two different samples of the Ascend 910C chip, the report noted.

Huawei was once in competition with Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) to be the world's biggest handset maker until U.S. restrictions, starting in 2019, began to curb its access to chip manufacturing tools needed to produce its most advanced models. However, in the past few years, Huawei has been making a comeback in the smartphone and processor segments. In August 2023, the company surprised many by quietly launching its new flagship smartphone, Mate 60 Pro. Last year, the company launched its flagship Mate 70 smartphone, Mate XT, and the Pura 70 series.

Last month, it was reported that Huawei plans to nearly double the production of its AI chip 910C in 2026 as it attempts to fill in the gaps left in the wake of Nvidia's (NVDA) dwindling shipments to the country.

Huawei’s current-generation accelerator 910C is ma ufactured by packaging two 910B dies together. The 910C hardware recently investigated by TechInsights, the report added.

China's Huawei has been able to use a stockpile of around 2.9 million dies in its Ascend chips and would have enough for the 910C through this year, the report added citing estimates from SemiAnalysis.

TSM said in a statement that, appears to be made with dies “analyzed by this organization in October of 2024, and not with a more recently manufactured die or more advanced technology. Shipments and manufacturing of that chip have been halted since then,” the report noted. 

TSM added that it complies with all export control rules and has not supplied Huawei since mid-September 2020, according to the report.

SK hynix is a major supplier of High-Bandwidth Memory, or HBM chips to Nvidia. Its rivals, to a lesser extent, include Micron Technology (MU) and Samsung.

In late 2024, the U.S. restricted sales to China of HBM2 and more advanced models and escalated curbs designed to limit the production capacity of Chinese chipmakers.

Like how Huawei was able to stockpile TSM logic wafer inventory, the company also stockpiled HBM inventory,” the report added citing SemiAnalysis.

It’s not clear when and how Huawei got the Samsung and SK hynix components, which both companies introduced years ago.

“SK hynix ceased all transactions with Huawei after the restrictions were placed in 2020,” SK hynix said in a statement, the report added. “SK Hynix is fully committed to strict compliance of all applicable laws and regulations, including US export regulations.” 

Samsung said it “continues to strictly comply” with U.S. export rules and does not have “any business relationships with entities outlined in the regulations," the report added.

SemiAnalysis said that China’s CXMT is making progress on HBM, but Huawei remains dependent on foreign hardware. SemiAnalysis noted that as the company depletes the stockpile, “we expect that China will be bottlenecked by HBM by the end of the year,” the report noted.
